en.extract:
The Madagascar jacana (Actophilornis albinucha) is a species of bird in the family Jacanidae. It is endemic to Madagascar.
It is not wary of human activity, but it is hypothesized to be avoidant of the common moorhen. It inhabits different sites within Madagascar during wet versus dry season. Female Madagascar jacanas are significantly larger than males.

The '''Madagascar jacana''' ('''''Actophilornis albinucha''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Jacanidae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Madagascar]].

It is not wary of human activity, but it is hypothesized to be avoidant of the [[common moorhen]]. It inhabits different sites within Madagascar during wet versus dry season. Female Madagascar jacanas are significantly larger than males.<ref name=":1">{{Cite journal |last1=Jackson |first1=Josephine D'Urban |last2=Zefania |first2=Sama |last3=Moehy |first3=Sebastien |last4=Bamford |first4=Andrew J |last5=Bruford |first5=Michael W |last6=Székely |first6=Tamás |date=2019-12-09 |title=Ecology, conservation, and phylogenetic position of the Madagascar Jacana ''Actophilornis albinucha'' |journal=Ostrich |volume=90 |issue=4 |pages=315–326 |bibcode=2019Ostri..90..315J |doi=10.2989/00306525.2019.1662508 |issn=0030-6525 |doi-access=free}}</ref>

zh.extract: 马岛雉鸻（学名：Actophilornis albinucha），又稱馬島水雉，是鸟纲鴴形目雉鸻科非洲雉鸻属的一种，分布于马达加斯加岛，故名。是当地的特有种。
